mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising consumer credit balances and increasing housing starts amplifies aggregate demand expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 20-day SPX returns, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor market conditions, an increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient corporate capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during high UNRATE periods, or the 60-day forward return correlation with INDPRO drops below 0.10 in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals strengthening economic momentum, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between quarters with positive GDPC1 growth versus negative growth (t-test p > 0.05), or the rolling 1-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds a threshold of 3.5 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation expectations force market participants to price in higher terminal rates, depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 3.5%, or the predictive Rank IC for CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 0.05 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in nonfarm payrolls growth and rise in the unemployment rate confirms a labor market cooling cycle, which reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction coefficient in a predictive model is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, a sharp rebound in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer confidence, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: HOUST rebounds do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns during post-shock periods, or the forward return differential between rebound and non-rebound months is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
